_description: Copy to state/repos.json and fill in. One entry per repo you want Hydra to poll. Hydra can work on external repos AND on itself simultaneously.

_trigger_modes.assignee: Commander picks up issues assigned to you (the gh auth user). Simplest UX — no labels required.
_trigger_modes.label: Commander picks up only issues labeled 'commander-ready'. Explicit per-ticket opt-in.
_trigger_modes.linear: Commander polls Linear via MCP. Requires 'claude mcp add linear' and state/linear.json populated.

repos._comment: DOGFOOD — Hydra working on Hydra itself. File issues on tonychang04/hydra (or your fork), assign to yourself, and let a worker implement a spec from docs/specs/. See DEVELOPING.md. `merge_policy` locks Hydra self-hosted to human-merge for every tier — framework changes are high-impact and irreversible on a live commander (per ticket #20). T3 is hardcoded `never` elsewhere; the key here is documentation only.
repos.name: hydra

repos._comment: Your own repo — the normal use case. `merge_policy` is optional: omit it to inherit the global policy.md defaults (T1 auto-merge, T2 human). The example here is the sweet-spot config for a team repo — auto-merge T1, auto-merge T2 only if worker-review returns 0 blockers (operator sees only PRs that needed judgment). Remove the merge_policy block entirely to fall back to the default. See docs/specs/2026-04-17-per-repo-merge-policy.md.
repos.name: your-repo

repos._comment: Another repo of yours, using the label trigger instead of assignee.
repos.name: another-repo

repos._comment: An infra-critical repo — disabled because it's effectively Tier 3 everywhere. Re-enable only if you want workers to attempt non-infra tickets in it.
repos.name: infra-repo

repos._comment: Cloud-commander example — on a Fly.io commander the repo is cloned to cloud_local_path by scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh before workers spawn. local_path stays set for parity with local-mode operators. git_url and cloud_local_path are both OPTIONAL — the defaults (https://github.com/<owner>/<name>.git and /hydra/data/repos/<owner>/<name>) are what most operators want. See docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-repo-clone.md.
repos.name: cloud-served-repo

notes: Flip enabled:false to exclude a repo temporarily. State labels (commander-working, commander-review, commander-stuck, commander-rejected, commander-reviewed, commander-skill-promotion) are created lazily by Hydra on first use — you do not need to create them up-front.
